steps.title: Identify the egocentric reference system
steps.content: The passage defines an egocentric frame as using left-right, body-based directions for time—left for past and right for future.

steps.title: Find what contradicts egocentric reference
steps.content:
Choice C shows the Yupno pointing towards an external landmark (the hill) for the future—backward if behind them, forward if ahead.

This reveals an allocentric (landmark-based) frame, not egocentric.

Choices A and B preserve left/right directional patterns, while D reverses the directions but maintains spatial reference.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
